finding_results.notes: The reveal now gives Kai and the reader a visible artifact before inference: the repeated `MERIDIAN ATHLETE FIRMWARE / INTERNAL` header and matching certificate ID appear in both columns, and Yusuf ties that package directly to the signed fix's server check. The scene also states the limit of proof: he cannot prove who inside Meridian authorized the design.
Tomas's blackout beat now stays in body-first athlete language: legs driving, arms cutting, sprint pattern holding, no subsystem or firmware jargon.
Kai's confrontation line now uses a Kai-specific stress tell: his voice goes low instead of flattening.
Only two explicit age mentions remain in the chapter.
The opening track beat and late confrontation no longer restate Kai's ache; the corridor line is the only retained wrist/shoulder ache passage.
Kai now enters the bay through its wrong, body-ready emptiness rather than a repeated processing-hum/habituation description.
The chapter keeps the strongest Birmingham beat at the doorway and rephrases later pressure beats through speed, polish loss, and stripped distance instead of renaming the accent shift.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_14.md:173; chapters/chapter_14.md:177; chapters/chapter_14.md:191; chapters/chapter_14.md:195; chapters/chapter_14.md:199; chapters/chapter_14.md:205; chapters/chapter_14.md:209; chapters/chapter_14.md:225; chapters/chapter_14.md:229
finding_results.notes: Roughened Yusuf's pressured speech across three passage clusters. Cluster 1 (lines 173, 177): added 'right' connective filler in the Farah explanation; replaced narrator-described speed ('the polish gone ragged') with audible pressure — self-interruption ('hasn't got — she hasn't got time'), repetition ('She hasn't got time'), and colloquial British register ('proper channels'). Cluster 2 (lines 191-209): recovered snapshot texture and amplified it — 'Right, so' filler, 'the bit that talks to' instead of formal label, 'it's the same bloody package, Kai' profanity under pressure, sentence piling with self-interruptions ('I can't —'), collapsed formal hedging ('Meaning somebody inside did, yeah' / 'Could be one team with access. Could be policy.'), colloquial 'phoning home' for server dependency, self-interruption in patch explanation ('so if they pull that — if they change the terms'), and recovered vivid phrasing from snapshot ('I can't prove someone sat in a room and said do this'). Cluster 3 (lines 225, 229): replaced literary narrator label ('stripped of the technical distance he usually kept') with terse 'Quieter now', added contraction ('bridge hardware's'), added mid-sentence self-correction ('The cord was already — it was degraded'), and contracted 'That's a full integration procedure'. At least three reply clusters now audibly shift out of polished professional cadence through interruption, self-correction, colloquial leakage, and pressure-language on the page rather than in narrator description.
Recast the blackout, impact, and feed-compression emphasis clusters into grammatically complete sentences while preserving the original causal ordering and physical violence.

Pulled Kai and Tomas back inside immediate observation and fear. Kai now reads visible stillness rather than narrating hidden damage, and Tomas's section registers panic, bodily silence, and economic dread without certifying permanence before the later medical and technical explanation.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised chapter 14 in place to keep Tomas's catastrophe body-close, make the Meridian link legible through a concrete on-screen artifact, align Kai's confrontation voice with the style bible, and reduce the chapter-local repetition/image-density flagged in the packet while preserving the chapter's revelation engine and ending state.

Single finding addressed: Yusuf's pressured dialogue in the Kai confrontation was too composed, with register shifts described by narrator rather than heard in the speech. Revised three passage clusters (Farah confession, virus-code explanation, bridge prognosis) to put Birmingham colloquial texture, self-interruptions, sentence piling, repetition under stress, and mild profanity into the lines themselves. Removed literary narrator labels that reported the register shift instead of showing it. Recovered and amplified spoken roughness from the pre-revision snapshot that had been smoothed away. All changes are local to Yusuf's dialogue; no structural, continuity, or heading-contract changes.

Addressed both prose/copyedit findings in chapter 14 by removing the flagged clipped-emphasis stacks and by restricting early injury narration to what Kai and Tomas can perceive or infer in the moment, while preserving the chapter's crash severity, payoff obligations, and continuity.
